signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.63 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Strait of Hormuz closure pushing WTI crude +3.05% to $101.1. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to safe-haven flows if VIX exceeds 25, but current VIX at 18.9 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above that handle. JPY safe-haven function is currently impaired as USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation (z=-1.90σ) breaks from norm.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) shows specs fading the hawkish RBNZ bias (market bias: Hike). With RBNZ at 2.25% and the next meeting on 27 May, a hawkish delivery or signal creates elevated short-squeeze risk for the crowded spec short. NZD/USD at 0.5947 is currently supported by the 1W G8 performance (+0.235%) despite the 150bp carry deficit vs the Fed.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7220 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 86% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the RBA's hiking cycle (4.35%). Institutional positioning is approaching the 80% extreme threshold; retail capitulation risk rises if price extends higher toward the 16 Jun RBA meeting. AUD carry premium of 60bp vs Fed supports the structural bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Macro + Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1749 — Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (175bp spread) favors USD, yet EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation has shifted to +0.707 (z=+1.65σ), indicating EUR is trading as a proxy for European equity sentiment rather than rate differentials. German ZEW and US CPI data today determine if the 1.1700 support holds. EUR COT LF net short -21,011 suggests neutral-to-bearish institutional bias ahead of the 11 Jun ECB meeting.
